what_changed: Atlantic basin buoy array registered sustained wind speed 148 km/h and central pressure 954 hPa; storm-track-model-nhc ingested readings and classified system as Category 3 hurricane on the Saffir-Simpson scale

storm-track-model-nhc generated ensemble of 42 projected landfall corridors; median track intersects coastal-zone-4 within 68-hour window with 62% confidence; uncertainty cone spans 280 km at predicted landfall

Tide gauge network recorded anomalous surge precursor of +0.4m above mean sea level along coastal-zone-4 shoreline, 61 hours ahead of projected landfall; surge-inundation-model flagged early resonance with shelf bathymetry

nhc-forecast-bulletin-14apr-0745 translated into Tropical Storm Watch for coastal-zone-4; national-meteorological-service issued public advisory NMS-ADV-001 specifying 68-hour landfall window and 62% confidence bound

public-advisory-nms-adv-001 relayed by media-broadcast-network across regional channels; framing emphasised 62% probability rather than 280 km uncertainty cone, compressing perceived confidence interval

coastal-resident-association recalled 2019 false alarm evacuation that caused 3 road fatalities and no storm; prior experience introduced institutional distrust; voluntary preparedness compliance rate estimated at 34% by community liaison

federal-emergency-management-agency activated pre-positioning protocol; shelter-database queried for coastal-zone-4 capacity — 4,200 registered beds across 7 facilities; road-capacity-model loaded current traffic baseline

hospital-administrator initiated patient census review: 312 patients in coastal-zone-4 facilities, 47 classified as non-ambulatory requiring specialised medical transport; medical-convoy-requirement-estimate logged as 12 dedicated vehicles

emergency-management-director requested mutual-aid agreement activation from two neighbouring districts to supplement medical transport fleet; request entered inter-agency coordination queue with 18-hour processing lag, blocking timely medical transport pre-positioning

local-mayor received NMS advisory and FEMA pre-positioning notice; legal counsel confirmed mandatory evacuation authority requires Category 3 landfall probability ≥70% within 48 hours per coastal-zone-ordinance-2021; current probability 62% at 68 hours — threshold not met

storm-track-model-nhc 06:00 UTC run ingested overnight dropsondes from NOAA reconnaissance flight; rapid intensification detected — central pressure dropped from 954 to 938 hPa in 11 hours; storm upgraded to Category 4

surge-inundation-model rerun with Category 4 parameters projected maximum inundation of 4.1m above mean sea level for coastal-zone-4 low-lying sectors; previous 3m threshold crossed — model output reclassified as life-threatening surge scenario
national-meteorological-service escalated from Tropical Storm Watch to Hurricane Warning for coastal-zone-4; landfall probability revised to 81% within 36 hours; NMS-WARNING-002 issued

federal-emergency-management-agency elevated response level to Level 2; emergency-management-director convened inter-agency call; road-capacity-model projected 22-hour clearance time for coastal-zone-4 population under voluntary compliance

local-mayor convened emergency session; probability now 81% at 36-hour window satisfies coastal-zone-ordinance-2021 threshold; political advisors flagged tourism revenue impact — 3 coastal resorts lobbied against mandatory order via chamber-of-commerce-letter-apr15

local-mayor issued voluntary evacuation recommendation for Zone A (lowest-lying sector, pop. 8,400); mandatory order held pending 14:00 briefing with emergency-management-director; media-broadcast-network aired recommendation as 'possible mandatory order imminent'

hospital-administrator confirmed mutual-aid-request-apr14 partially fulfilled: 8 of 12 required medical transport vehicles secured; 4-vehicle gap remains; non-ambulatory patient evacuation timeline now extends to 19 hours from order issuance

shelter-database queried against projected voluntary compliance (estimated 34%): 1,430 residents anticipated at shelters, within 4,200-bed capacity; however road-capacity-model projected traffic surge beginning T-30h would reduce effective capacity by 18% due to contra-flow setup time

storm-track-model-nhc 00:00 UTC run confirmed rapid intensification continuing; track shifted 40 km northward placing coastal-zone-4 urban core directly in projected eyewall; landfall probability revised to 94% within 24 hours

local-mayor signed mandatory evacuation order for Zones A–C (pop. 41,000) effective 08:00 local time; coastal-zone-ordinance-2021 authority invoked; order transmitted to federal-emergency-management-agency, media-broadcast-network, and coastal-resident-association simultaneously

media-broadcast-network broadcast mandatory-evacuation-order-zones-abc on all regional channels with zone maps and shelter locations; emergency alert system activated; coastal-resident-association fractured — 22% of members publicly stated intent to shelter-in-place citing prior false alarm

road-capacity-model activated contra-flow protocol on highway-coast-91; projected clearance time 18 hours from 08:00 order activation, placing last vehicles inland by 02:00 on 2026-04-17 — 4 hours before projected landfall

shelter-database registered intake at 6 of 7 facilities at T+0h; Facility-3 flagged structural inspection backlog — building had failed 2024 wind-load assessment but remediation was deferred pending budget approval; facility removed from active shelter list by emergency-management-director

hospital-administrator initiated non-ambulatory patient evacuation convoy; 4-vehicle gap partially resolved — 2 additional vehicles sourced from military-reserve-pool; 2 patients requiring ventilator transport still unallocated; transfer window closure estimated at T+16h

utility-grid-operator initiated pre-emptive rolling shutdown of coastal-zone-4 grid sectors per emergency-grid-protocol-2023; shutdown sequence began with non-critical infrastructure; estimated full de-energisation by T+6h to prevent post-storm electrocution risk from downed lines

shelter-capacity-revised-16apr (3,780 beds after Facility-3 removal) crossed 80% occupancy threshold at T+3h45m; shelter-database triggered overflow protocol — overflow-shelter-highschool-inland activated, adding 320 beds; total capacity now 4,100

local-mayor received compliance report from emergency-management-director: 76% of Zone A–C residents departed; 24% shelter-in-place; enforcement closure report drafted but blocked pending signature from both federal-emergency-management-agency regional director and state-governor-office — neither official reachable within standard communications window

meshgraph:a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4000-8000-000000000001 produced by articulation of meteorological-analyst cut at T-72h entered mesh as source; the act of observing the storm network became itself a node in the coordination network — partial closure of Principle 8 loop, pending M7-B
